main.description: Plant a seed — publish a new root bud by redeeming an ink.branchline.seedGrant the caller holds. The AppView verifies: (1) the grant exists and its CID matches; (2) the caller's DID equals the grant's `recipient`; (3) the chain of predecessor grants walks back to a root held by a trusted grantor, with every hop's `holder.did == previous.recipient`; (4) no grant in the chain is revoked or already committed in a prior bud. On success the AppView writes an ink.branchline.bud record referencing the grant to the caller's PDS and atomically commits every grant in the chain in its local ledger so the chain cannot be spent again. Rejection reasons are named below. Continuing an existing bud (replies) uses ink.branchline.publishBud instead.

grantUri.description: AT-URI of the ink.branchline.seedGrant being redeemed. Must name a grant whose current recipient is the caller.

errors.name: Unauthorized
errors.description: The caller is not signed in.

errors.name: GrantNotFound
errors.description: No ink.branchline.seedGrant record exists at `grantUri`, or it could not be fetched from its holder's PDS.

errors.name: GrantNotYours
errors.description: The caller's DID does not match the grant's `recipient`. Only the recipient may plant or gift a grant.

errors.name: GrantRevoked
errors.description: The grant, or a predecessor in its chain, has been deleted from its holding repo before being committed into a bud.

errors.name: GrantAlreadySpent
errors.description: The grant, or a predecessor in its chain, has already been committed into a different bud. Grants are single-use.

errors.name: ChainBroken
errors.description: The grant chain failed validation — a holder's DID did not match the preceding grant's recipient, or the chain exceeded the maximum depth.

errors.name: UntrustedGrantor
errors.description: The chain does not terminate at a root grant held by a DID the AppView trusts to mint seed grants.

errors.name: WordLimitExceeded
errors.description: The bud text exceeds the 500-word ceiling.
